items.title: Why Hollywood Took Over the Film Industry
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
Why is America the centre of the film industry? Or more specifically, why is Hollywood the centre of the film industry? How did a small suburb transform into the bustling hub of entertainment we see today? In this video we're looking at how Hollywood and America took over the film industry. We'll be going back in time, talking about patents, geography, the world wars, and more!

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:42 - The Birth of Cinema
02:28 - Rise of the Film Industry
03:56 - America's Rise
05:42 - Why Hollywood?
08:09 - The Studio System
10:02 - Post World War II Expansion
11:33 - Other Film Industries

items.title: When an Actor Fights for a Comeback - Tom Hardy's Career
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
For Tom Hardy, success didn’t come easy, it was a battle. From nearly losing it all after his first big role in Star Trek Nemesis, to reinventing himself with an intensity few actors can match, Hardy’s journey has been anything but ordinary.

Whether he’s packing on muscle for Bronson and The Dark Knight Rises, breaking bones in Warrior, or surviving chaotic productions like Mad Max: Fury Road and The Revenant, Hardy goes all in. Even when he’s just sitting alone in a car for an entire film like in Locke. He’s one of the most committed actors of his generation that always steals the scene. But these days he seems to just be having fun with it, starring in the Venom films, taking on more creative roles, practicing Brazilian jiu-jitsu, and even rapping?

In this video, we’re diving into the turning points of Tom Hardy’s career, his setbacks, his relentless rise, his toughest productions, and the reason you don’t see him in as many films as before. This is the story of an actor who fought his way back, this is Tom Hardy.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:56 - Rise to Fame & Flop
04:27 - Comeback
09:49 - 2015 - His Best Year
14:16 - Creative and Chilling

Tom Hardy. Leonardo DiCaprio. Best Actor. Intense Actor. Inception. Mad Max Fury Road. Dunkirk. Christopher Nolan. The Dark Knight Rises. Bane. Venom. Venom Carnage The Last Dance. The Revenant.
